upstream_evidence.note: Hello handling depends on identify-populated protocol state.
Discovery flow evaluates identify protocol support for hello and chain exchange.

py_libp2p_evidence.note: Runs identify and reports advertised Filecoin protocol support.

rows.current_practical_support: Supported for peer diagnostics and advertised protocol inspection against Filecoin peers.
failure_modes: Advertised protocol support does not imply full Filecoin hello or chain exchange runtime behavior.
rows.recommended_next_step: Keep identify as the primary diagnostics surface and track runtime protocol parity separately.

upstream_evidence.note: Forest records ping results in the libp2p service.

py_libp2p_evidence.note: Runs ping and reports RTT summaries.

rows.current_practical_support: Supported for connectivity and latency diagnostics against Filecoin peers.
failure_modes: Ping validates transport reachability only and does not prove Filecoin protocol interoperability.
rows.recommended_next_step: Keep ping in the diagnostics toolchain without widening its scope.

upstream_evidence.note: Lotus hello protocol constant.
Forest hello protocol constant.

py_libp2p_evidence.note: Exports FIL_HELLO_PROTOCOL.

rows.current_practical_support: The hello protocol ID is available for diagnostics and protocol-list inspection.
failure_modes: Constant parity alone does not provide the hello request/response behavior.
rows.recommended_next_step: Keep the constant stable and use it as the anchor for future hello behavior work.

upstream_evidence.note: Lotus exchanges typed hello and latency messages over CBOR.
Forest wraps hello request/response behavior and timeouts.
Forest defines typed hello request and response messages.

py_libp2p_evidence.note: Current demo checks advertised protocol support only.

rows.current_practical_support: py-libp2p can detect hello support but cannot speak the hello wire protocol today.
failure_modes: No hello codec or request/response service.
No genesis or heaviest-tipset validation path.
No latency response handling.
rows.recommended_next_step: Implement a typed hello codec and request/response behavior before claiming runtime interoperability.

upstream_evidence.note: Lotus chain exchange protocol constant.
Forest chain exchange protocol constant.

py_libp2p_evidence.note: Exports FIL_CHAIN_EXCHANGE_PROTOCOL.

rows.current_practical_support: The chain exchange protocol ID is available for diagnostics and protocol-list inspection.
failure_modes: Constant parity alone does not provide chain exchange request/response support.
rows.recommended_next_step: Keep the protocol ID stable and track runtime behavior separately.

upstream_evidence.note: Lotus serves validated chain exchange responses.
Lotus validates and consumes chain exchange responses.
Forest wires chain exchange into request/response behavior.
Forest defines typed chain exchange request and response messages.

py_libp2p_evidence.note: Current demo checks advertised protocol support only.

rows.current_practical_support: py-libp2p can confirm that a peer advertises chain exchange but does not implement the typed request/response behavior.
failure_modes: No chain exchange codec or message models.
No server/provider path.
No client-side response validation path.
rows.recommended_next_step: Treat full chain exchange as a dedicated follow-up module rather than broadening Module 2 beyond diagnostics.

upstream_evidence.note: Lotus configures Filecoin gossipsub defaults.
Forest builds gossipsub behavior with Filecoin defaults.

py_libp2p_evidence.note: Exports the Filecoin gossipsub protocol preference set.
Observer snapshot reports the negotiated Filecoin-oriented protocol set.

rows.current_practical_support: The Filecoin pubsub preset exposes the /meshsub/2.0.0, 1.2.0, 1.1.0, and 1.0.0 family expected by Filecoin nodes.
failure_modes: Negotiation parity does not imply full scoring, gater, or allowlist parity.
rows.recommended_next_step: Keep protocol negotiation stable and track deeper pubsub behavior gaps separately.

upstream_evidence.note: Lotus shared helpers define blocks, messages, and DHT naming.
Forest subscribes to Filecoin block and message topics by network suffix.

py_libp2p_evidence.note: Exposes blocks, messages, and DHT helper functions.
Maps network aliases to the effective genesis-network suffix.
CLI surfaces the effective topics and DHT name.

rows.current_practical_support: Topic and DHT naming are fully available for mainnet and calibnet, including the historical mainnet->testnetnet mapping.
rows.recommended_next_step: Keep the helper layer pinned to upstream snapshots and update it only after explicit parity review.

upstream_evidence.note: Lotus derives message IDs from Blake2b-256 over payload bytes.
Forest configures gossipsub message IDs from Blake2b-256(data).

py_libp2p_evidence.note: Defines filecoin_message_id().
Installs filecoin_message_id() into the Filecoin pubsub preset.

rows.current_practical_support: The Filecoin DX layer uses the expected Blake2b-256(data) message ID strategy.
failure_modes: Message ID parity does not validate Filecoin block or message semantics.
rows.recommended_next_step: Keep this function stable as the message identity anchor for Filecoin-compatible observation.

upstream_evidence.note: Lotus score thresholds.
Forest score thresholds.

py_libp2p_evidence.note: Exports the threshold constants.
Builds the Filecoin score params from those thresholds.
filecoin-dx preset reports the effective threshold values.

rows.current_practical_support: The Filecoin DX layer exposes the same threshold values for publish, gossip, graylist, accept-PX, and opportunistic graft decisions.
failure_modes: Threshold parity alone does not deliver upstream topic-score behavior.
rows.recommended_next_step: Keep filecoin-dx preset as the authoritative runtime snapshot for the current score surface.

upstream_evidence.note: Lotus configures richer topic scoring, peer gater, and allowlist behavior.
Forest carries topic-score parameters for Filecoin topics.

py_libp2p_evidence.note: Stores upstream topic-score constants but only applies threshold gating at runtime.
Observer demo reports its compatibility limits explicitly.

rows.current_practical_support: Runtime behavior is intentionally limited to threshold gating plus reference constants for future work.
failure_modes: No peer gater parity.
No subscription allowlist parity.
No runtime use of the full topic-score reference values.
rows.recommended_next_step: Add richer scoring only when the project is ready to own the larger behavioral and test surface.

upstream_evidence.note: Lotus changes mesh/PX behavior for bootstrappers and supports direct peers.
Forest redials bootstrap peers at the service layer.

py_libp2p_evidence.note: build_filecoin_gossipsub() exposes bootstrapper and direct-peer inputs.
Runtime bootstrap helpers adapt canonical Filecoin bootstrap lists.

rows.current_practical_support: py-libp2p supports bootstrapper-flavored gossipsub presets, PX toggles, direct peers, and runtime bootstrap address selection.
failure_modes: No peer-gater parity.
No Lotus-style allowlist behavior.
No service-level bootstrapper behavior beyond address helpers.
rows.recommended_next_step: Treat deeper bootstrapper semantics as a second-stage pubsub parity effort.

upstream_evidence.note: Lotus adds drand and optional F3 topics to its pubsub surface.
Forest corpus provided here focuses on Filecoin block/message topics.

py_libp2p_evidence.note: Reference constants include drand topic-score values only.
Observer demo intentionally limits itself to Filecoin block/message topics.

rows.current_practical_support: The current DX layer intentionally stops at block/message topic support.
failure_modes: No drand topic construction path.
No F3 topic support.
No allowlist or validation plumbing for these adjacent topics.
rows.recommended_next_step: Keep drand and F3 as a later expansion once the core Filecoin block/message flow is stable.

upstream_evidence.note: Forest includes bitswap in its broader libp2p service.

py_libp2p_evidence.note: Module 5 positioning deliberately scoped the Filecoin DX layer away from broader protocol claims.

rows.current_practical_support: Module 2 does not make Filecoin-specific bitswap claims; it remains focused on diagnostics, hello/chain exchange parity gaps, and Filecoin pubsub compatibility.
rows.recommended_next_step: Track Filecoin-specific bitswap questions in a separate interoperability or performance module if they become necessary.
